metadata.description: Skills for AI coding agents — review, research, UI/UX audit, MCP & framework builders, browser/device automation, config files, publish. Install the whole pack, a themed bundle, a single skill, or just the researcher agents.

plugins.name: yk-everything
plugins.description: The whole pack — all 43 skills plus the internet-researcher agents. Heaviest context cost; prefer a themed bundle or single skill.

plugins.name: yk-researchers
plugins.description: Internet-researcher subagents only (no skills) — api-docs, debug-stuck, tech-choice, shipping-pattern, quick, generic. Fan them out for source-backed answers.

plugins.name: yk-review
plugins.description: Review & completion — code review, review-feedback triage, done-claim audits, runtime debugging.

plugins.name: yk-frontend
plugins.description: Frontend rebuild & audit — pixel-faithful URL→Next.js, UI/UX/Laws-of-UX audits.

plugins.name: yk-mcp
plugins.description: MCP & agent interfaces — build (SDK v1/v2, mcp-use), clean architecture, convert v1→v2, test, and agent-readiness audits for MCP servers and CLIs.

plugins.name: yk-build
plugins.description: App & framework builders — Chrome MV3, Cloudflare Email Service, Effect-TS v3, Kernel SDK, LangChain.js, Raycast, TinaCMS+Next.js.

plugins.name: yk-research
plugins.description: Research & discovery — single-question and wave-based corpus research, GitHub repo scouting, bulk Codex search. Bundles the internet-researcher subagents.

plugins.name: yk-automation
plugins.description: Live automation — agent-browser CLI, Android device control, Tailscale Funnel public tunnels.

plugins.name: yk-config
plugins.description: Instruction & config files — AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/REVIEW.md hierarchies, drift audits, scenario Makefiles.

plugins.name: yk-ops
plugins.description: Ops & release — Railway, Coolify Cloud compose deploys, repo cleanup, npm publishing.

plugins.name: yk-skills
plugins.description: Skill authoring — research-driven skill creation and derailment stress-testing of existing SKILL.md files.

plugins.name: yk-delivery
plugins.description: Aligned delivery — scored multi-round question alignment, a filename-state spec corpus, and waved parallel subagent orchestration for a large or ambiguous initiative.

plugins.name: audit-agentic-cli
plugins.description: Use skill if you are auditing or designing a CLI for agent/LLM consumption — stable JSON output, exit codes, non-interactive flags, or iterative repair loops for machine-generated artifacts.

plugins.name: audit-agentic-mcp
plugins.description: Use skill if you are auditing an existing MCP server for agent-readiness, designing a new MCP server before any code, or deciding framework, security, and context posture.

plugins.name: audit-completion
plugins.description: Use skill if you are verifying claimed-done work, auditing session/plan/branch completion, finding partial or assumed-complete tasks, or answering "are we done?" with evidence.

plugins.name: audit-skill-by-derailment
plugins.description: Use skill if you are testing or hardening an existing SKILL.md by running a fresh subagent on a real task and editing the skill where the trace shows friction.

plugins.name: audit-ui-and-save-files
plugins.description: Use skill if you are auditing a running web app's UI across pages and viewports, saving per-bug findings to a dated tree, and dispatching approval-gated fix subagents.

plugins.name: audit-ux-and-save-files
plugins.description: Use skill if you are auditing a running app's usability from real personas walking their journeys, saving per-issue findings to a dated persona/journey tree and recommending major changes, not fixes.

plugins.name: audit-ux-laws
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or auditing UI against the 30 Laws of UX — Fitts's, Hick's, Miller's, Jakob's, Gestalt, choice overload, or cognitive load.

plugins.name: build-chrome-extension
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or debugging a Chrome MV3 extension — manifest.json v3, service_worker, content_scripts, chrome.runtime/storage/alarms, popup, side_panel, declarativeNetRequest, or Web Store packaging.

plugins.name: build-clean-mcp-architecture
plugins.description: Use skill if you are placing, refactoring, or auditing TypeScript mcp-use/server code under src/ and need Clean Architecture layer boundaries, folder layout, import direction, or dependency-cruiser gates.

plugins.name: build-cloudflare-email-sending
plugins.description: Use skill if you are migrating email to Cloudflare Email Service, replacing Resend, SES, Postmark, or Supabase Auth mailer, wiring a send_email Worker binding, or architecting Cloudflare email sending.

plugins.name: build-effect-ts-v3
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building TypeScript code with Effect-TS v3 — effect@3.x, @effect/*, Effect.gen, Layer, Schema, typed errors, fibers, Stream.

plugins.name: build-kernel-ts-sdk
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building TypeScript browser-automation agents or apps on the Kernel SDK (`@onkernel/sdk`) — browsers, Apps, profiles, Managed Auth, pools, Playwright/CDP wiring.

plugins.name: build-langchain-ts-app
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building TypeScript apps that import `langchain` or `@langchain/*` for agents, tool-calling, RAG retrievers, structured output, streaming, or LangGraph `StateGraph` workflows.

plugins.name: build-mcp-server-sdk-v1
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or maintaining a TypeScript MCP server on @modelcontextprotocol/sdk v1.x — single-package, Zod schemas, RequestHandlerExtra, McpServer.

plugins.name: build-mcp-server-sdk-v2
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or maintaining MCP servers on @modelcontextprotocol/server v2 alpha — split packages, McpServer, registerTool, ctx.mcpReq handler context.

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-agent
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or auditing TypeScript mcp-use MCPAgent code where an LLM picks and orchestrates MCP tools via run, stream, or streamEvents.

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-client
plugins.description: Use skill if you are writing TypeScript MCP client code with mcp-use — MCPClient, MCPSession, useMcp, McpClientProvider, mcp-use/browser, mcp-use/react, or npx mcp-use client.

plugins.name: build-mcp-use-server
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building TypeScript MCP servers with mcp-use/server — server.tool, response helpers, ctx.auth, sessions, transports, widgets, Inspector, deploy.

plugins.name: build-raycast-script-command
plugins.description: Use skill if you are authoring or fixing a Raycast Script Command (.sh or .py with @raycast.* metadata header) and need correct fields, modes, arguments, or discovery.

plugins.name: build-skill
plugins.description: Use skill if you are creating a new Claude skill, redesigning one, or merging multiple skills, and need evidence-based research and comparison before writing SKILL.md.

plugins.name: build-tinacms-nextjs
plugins.description: Use skill if you are building or extending a TinaCMS-backed Next.js App Router site with tina/config.ts, MDX/git-backed content, schema modeling, useTina visual editing, or TinaCloud/self-hosted deploys.

plugins.name: convert-mcp-sdk-v1-to-v2
plugins.description: Use skill if you are porting an existing MCP TypeScript server from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk v1.x to the v2 split-package SDK with package renames, ServerContext, and Zod v4.

plugins.name: convert-url-to-nextjs
plugins.description: Use skill if you are rebuilding a deployed site as a Next.js project AS-IS pixel-faithful from a live URL, lost-frontend repo recovery, or .html snapshot.

plugins.name: debug-runtime
plugins.description: Use skill if you are chasing a reproducible runtime bug, intermittent failure, or repeated failed fixes and need a language-agnostic 4-phase root-cause workflow before editing product code.

plugins.name: deploy-coolify-cloud
plugins.description: Use skill if you are deploying or updating a docker-compose service on Coolify Cloud (app.coolify.io) via its API — setting domains, connecting services, env secrets, or verifying a deploy.

plugins.name: init-agent-config
plugins.description: Use skill if you are creating, auditing, or migrating CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or REVIEW.md instruction files, folder-scoped agent guidance, or cross-agent companion entrypoints.

plugins.name: init-makefiles
plugins.description: Use skill if you are scaffolding or refreshing Makefile targets for local dev, tunnels, deploys, R2 sync, Supabase, Railway, Vercel, or Mac shipping.

plugins.name: mobilerun-control
plugins.description: Use skill if you are controlling, automating, or testing a connected Android phone via the mobilerun CLI — tapping, typing, swiping, reading the screen, or running a multi-step on-device task.

plugins.name: publish-npm-package
plugins.description: Use skill if you are publishing to npm via GitHub Actions release workflow with trusted publishing, NPM_TOKEN, provenance, semantic-release, changesets, release-please, or fixing npm publish CI.

plugins.name: run-agent-browser
plugins.description: Use skill if you are driving the agent-browser CLI for ad hoc browser automation — @ref snapshots, sessions, forms, extraction, screenshots, headed/stealth, or provider runs.

plugins.name: run-aligned-delivery
plugins.description: Use skill if you are driving a large, ambiguous initiative (modernize, rewrite, migrate, greenfield, or a multi-component bug hunt) and want scored multi-round question alignment before orchestrating parallel execution.

plugins.name: run-codex-adversarial-loop
plugins.description: Use skill if you are fanning out many parallel Codex adversarial reviews across derived lenses, independently verifying every finding, fixing confirmed issues in grouped worktrees, and looping until clean.

plugins.name: run-codex-review-loop
plugins.description: Use skill if you are running Codex review loops across current branches, multiple lenses, comparisons, convergence rounds, or saved review runs.

plugins.name: run-deep-research
plugins.description: Use skill if you are running deep multi-file research over 5+ entities or a market/category — a wave-dispatched evidence corpus on disk, executed by Claude subagents or codex exec.

plugins.name: run-github-scout
plugins.description: Use skill if you are discovering GitHub repos, shortlisting candidates for a concrete need, or comparing OSS projects with adaptive search and repo evidence.

plugins.name: run-railway
plugins.description: Use skill if you are running railway CLI commands for deploys, logs, env vars, link, ssh, db shells, scaling, or reconciling installed CLI vs upstream docs.

plugins.name: run-repo-cleanup
plugins.description: Use skill if you are finishing a project and want every live branch and worktree reviewed and merged into main locally, dangling branches retired, and junk swept aside.

plugins.name: run-research
plugins.description: Use skill if you are answering one technical research question with current web evidence, Reddit practitioner experience, and source-backed synthesis, optionally fanned out across parallel subagents.

plugins.name: run-review
plugins.description: Use skill if you are reviewing a PR or diff, asking for review on your branch, triaging received review feedback, or delegating review to codex.

plugins.name: run-tailscale-funnel
plugins.description: Use skill if you are exposing a local HTTP server at a public `.ts.net` URL via Tailscale Funnel for agent-browser navigation, mobile testing, webhooks, or shared dev demos.

plugins.name: search-it-bulk-by-codex
plugins.description: Use skill if you are running many small Codex-native web searches through codex exec with per-question files and parseable answer artifacts.

plugins.name: test-by-mcpc-cli
plugins.description: Use skill if you are driving the mcpc CLI (0.2.x) to test, debug, or smoke-check an MCP server over stdio or Streamable HTTP.

plugins.name: update-agent-config
plugins.description: Use skill if you are auditing AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/REVIEW.md hierarchies for drift after refactors: stale file:line refs, frequency-table recounts, invalidated rules, or missing AGENTS.md for code-bearing gap folders.
